steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which inequality matches a shaded region on a graph. Read the slope and $y$-intercept of the boundary line, then determine the inequality direction from which side is shaded.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
The boundary line crosses the $y$-axis at $-35$ and rises steeply: rise/run = $25$, not $5$. So the boundary is $y = 25x - 35$. The shading is above the line, so $y \gt $.
  • Choice A — $y \gt 5x - 35$: wrong slope ($5$ instead of $25$). ✗
  • Choice B — $y \lt 5x - 35$: wrong slope and wrong direction. ✗
  • Choice C — $y \gt 25x - 35$: correct slope and correct direction (shading above). ✓
  • Choice D — $y \lt 25x - 35$: correct slope but wrong direction. ✗
C is correct.
